authority_profile_note: The operator authority profile mechanism now exists, so the emitted authority is operator-configured (no longer a provisional wizard default) and carries no pending re-emit trigger (expires_on_trigger: none). The operator's operative autonomy is the derived AUTONOMY_LEVEL foundation-doc field plus the per-cell authority gates; the top-level posture here is the conservative baseline the inherited cells are stamped under. Deriving the top-level posture per-operator from the profile (rather than the conservative baseline) is a documented follow-on and does not change AUTONOMY_LEVEL or the per-cell gates.
model_tiers_note: Neutral default. The real tier-to-model resolution is supplied at generation time via an override; the placeholder strings here keep the distributed data shape-correct without pinning a model name that changes over time.
i9_coverage_note: The minimal set of emitted paths needed to satisfy the emission plan's path-coverage invariant (every control-plane path and agent output directory must be a covered emitted file or a declared runtime-created path). This is NOT the full output manifest — the complete manifest is computed by scanning the generated tree.

agent_output_profile.permitted_write_directories_note: The agent's task-output dir PLUS the control-plane operational paths every agent prompt mandates writing to (its checkpoint plan, its handoff envelope, three-strikes escalation to issues_log, and operational/audit logging). The agent's blast-radius hard gate checks declared writes against this set, so it MUST cover everything the prompt instructs — narrowing it to the output dir alone halts the agent on its first checkpoint write. Specific log files (not the whole logs/ dir) keep the boundary minimal.

allowed_resource_claims_note: Only claims with a deterministic generation effect are allowed. A claimed capability the shape does not support is rejected by the assembler, never silently dropped.
